message:
feat(search): replace BM25 with single-pass precomputed scorer as primary ranking engine

The BM25 tiebreaker added latency without meaningfully improving ranking
quality — the multi-signal scorer already handles exact, prefix, and
substring matching. This commit promotes the scorer to be the sole
ranking engine and adds several new matching capabilities:

- Precomputed ScorerIndex with Set-based O(1) exact lookups so the index
  can be built once and reused across queries
- Proportional prefix scoring (longer prefixes score closer to exact)
- Levenshtein fuzzy matching with edit-distance thresholds (1 edit for
  4-6 char tokens, 2 edits for 7+ chars) for typo tolerance
- Phrase adjacency bonus rewarding consecutive query terms at consecutive
  field positions
- Acronym matching ("rmm" finds "Robot Mode Maker")
- Exact-ID boost (query "idea-wizard" jumps to #1 for that prompt)
- Native synonym expansion inside the scorer with a discount factor so
  direct matches always rank above synonym-derived matches
- Query token deduplication to prevent score inflation

The engine.ts is now a thin filter layer (category/tags/limit) over
searchScorerIndex(). CLI commands updated to use buildScorerIndex instead
of the removed buildIndex (BM25), and suggest.ts recalibrated its
relevance-percent mapping from BM25's 0-5 range to the scorer's 1-50
range. Tests expanded from ~190 to ~440 lines covering every new signal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
